zh.lifeSummary: 當Cecily de Beauchamp於1321年出生在英國薩默塞特陶頓時，她的父親Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset17歲，母親Margaret Porter17歲。她於大約1340年與Gilbert de Turberville d.s.p.結婚。她去世於1394年6月7日在英國英格蘭薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘享年73歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Cecily
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

variants.name: Cecilie
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

variants.name: Cicely
meanings: Variant of   Cecily .  This was a common form of the name in the Middle Ages, and in the 20th century was well known as the name of the British actress and singer  Cicely Courtneidge  (  1893–1980 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Cecily de Beauchamp was born in 1321, in Taunton, Somerset, England, her father, Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset, was 17 and her mother, Margaret Porter, was 17. She married Gilbert de Turberville d.s.p. about 1340. She died on 7 June 1394, in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 73.

Elle a épousé Gilbert de Turberville d.s.p. environ 1340. Elle est décédée le 7 juin 1394, à Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 73 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Cecily de Beauchamp(이)가 1321년 영국 서머세트 타운턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset(은)는 17세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Porter(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 대략 1340년 Gilbert de Turberville d.s.p.(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1394년 6월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 해트치 보챔프에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Cecily de Beauchamp родилась в 1321 г. в местности таунтон, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Porter, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gilbert de Turberville d.s.p. около 1340 г. Она умерла 7 июня 1394 г. в местности хач бочамп, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 73 лет.
